proof_trace: Port buffers allocated in q6asm_map_memory_regions (line 516) via kzalloc are stored in ac->port[dir].buf but never freed in q6asm_audio_client_release. The release function only frees the main audio_client structure, leaking the port buffer allocations.
attack_scenario: Attacker repeatedly allocates audio clients with port buffers and calls q6asm_audio_client_free, causing port buffer memory to accumulate until system memory exhaustion occurs.

proof_trace: Graph resources allocated by q6apm_graph_open() (line 257) are not freed on error paths after line 257. Error cleanup only calls kfree(prtd) but missing required q6apm_graph_close(prtd->graph) as done in normal cleanup.
attack_scenario: Attacker triggers PCM constraint failures after q6apm_graph_open succeeds, causing repeated graph resource leaks leading to memory exhaustion.
reason: Confirmed new TP in default Sonnet 4.5 run

proof_trace: Memory allocated by audioreach_alloc_graph_pkt() at line 66 and stored in graph->graph is not freed when idr_alloc() fails at line 75. The error path at line 78 only calls kfree(graph) but omits kfree(graph->graph), causing a memory leak.
attack_scenario: An attacker can repeatedly trigger idr_alloc failures by exhausting the ID space, causing memory leaks with each failure that could lead to system memory exhaustion.
